plot_context_observations: Action/espionage and franchise set-pieces (multiple Mission: Impossible entries; Jack Ryan; Tom Cruise recurring)
Science-fiction focus on digital/virtual worlds and AI (Tron, Tron: Ares, Tron: Legacy; The Terminator; Dune; Mickey 17)
Revenge and vigilantism arcs (Sisu: Road to Revenge; Lady Snowblood; Monkey Man; One Battle After Another)
War, legal aftermath and historical dramas (Nuremberg; Band of Brothers; The American Revolution; Chief of War)
Post-apocalyptic and survival scenarios (Fallout; The Last of Us; 28 Days Later; Prospect)
Family, grief and intimate character drama (Hamnet; Hereditary; If I Had Legs I'd Kick You; Titanic)
Crime, police and institutional-corruption stories (End of Watch; U-571; Blue Lights; Task)
Dark/genre-blending horror and mystery (The Black Phone; Hereditary; Saw; Weapons)
Musical/album consumption alongside films (Pink Floyd album tracks and concert/album content present)
viewing_summary.executive_summary: Recent viewing skews toward high-energy action, science fiction (including virtual/digital worlds), and prestige drama — a mix of blockbuster/franchise binges (Mission: Impossible, Lord of the Rings, Tron) and auteur-driven releases (Nolan, Bong, Chloé Zhao, Paul Thomas Anderson). The history also shows interest in revenge narratives, war/historical dramas, survival/post-apocalyptic stories, and occasional comedy/rom-com and music listening (Pink Floyd, Santana).
source_gaps: Cinematographer credits are absent or empty for nearly all items in the provided history — cannot report on DP preferences.

Some entries lack metadata (e.g., missing summary, cast or director for items like 'Warfare', 'Beauty and the Beast', 'The Man in the High Castle', 'The Rehearsal'); conclusions drawn only from available fields.
Cinematographer metadata was not present in the supplied Plex items.
